threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s a good idea to get a taxi after midnight instead of the bus—trust me, the bus is a rite of passage here.
Heard a bloke on the 36 bus last night complaining about his mate's 'horrendous sock tan'. Only in Peckham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids setting up a pop-up café in the park with no adults around, just their giggles and a makeshift sign. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find spontaneous open-air jam sessions by the canal—no permits, just pure creativity spilling out into the night.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they instinctively know the best time to avoid BART delays and can name all the ghost kitchens in the area.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a kid try to catch a Muni bus by jogging alongside it, only to trip and fall. Bus driver just smirked and rolled on, like it was an everyday show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: You'd think tourists would know tapas are a snack, not a meal. Watching them order a dozen and wonder why they can't move after is wild.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewildered look while trying to navigate the Santiago Rusiñol square – it’s a maze, not a shortcut.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'Shoreditch Effect' is just a trendy art show, not a reason to raise rent prices across Stoke Newington.
On the 75 last night, a lad argued passionately with his kebab about the meaning of life. The bus driver just chuckled and kept driving.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I caught the scent of freshly baked lahmacun wafting from the corner bakery at 3 AM last weekend. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst at 4 AM and still hear someone passionately debating the latest techno festival during your midnight snack.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively know to avoid BART delays and plan their evenings around it, not just complain about it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a pet parrot on the 19 Polk—parrot was squawking about the weather while everyone else was glued to their phones. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a coffee at 4 PM without realizing most locals are on the 'tapa' circuit by then, not sitting in cafés.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their overly enthusiastic 'Hola!' from a distance, while locals stick to discreet nods and brief smiles.
